name: Variety of genus (and species) — includes 'Select class'
urlObserved: CONFIRMED verbatim: https://importnotification-static-snd.azure.defra.cloud/notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011/commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000 — trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 request 888 (GET 200, 6.4K). The page GETs and POSTs to the SAME URL (the <form> carries no action attribute — independently verified: document.querySelector('form').getAttribute('action') === null).
pageTitle: Variety of Genus (and Species) - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Description of the goods
heading: Variety and class of commodity
bodyCopyNotes: The page has NO visible body copy in the observed default state — independently re-verified against main.innerText, which reads exactly: 'Description of the goods / Variety and class of commodity / CIDAC - Citrus australasica / Variety\tClass\t / Remove button / None / Select class / Class I / Class II / Extra Class / Add another variety / Save and continue Add another Genus (and species)'. Two kinds of conditional copy can appear and are deliberately NOT listed here as bodyCopy: (a) the 'Please ensure this variety is not in the dropdown list' warning, which is js-hidden inside the table <caption> and only revealed when variety = 'Other' (see govukComponents > Warning text); (b) one of three mutually-exclusive max-commodity-lines advisory paragraphs (see validationMessages). A rebuild reading bodyCopy must not render any of these unconditionally.
nextPage: /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/:draftId/commodity/summary — the CHED-PP Commodity summary page (pageTitle 'Commodity - Import and export applications - GOV.UK', H1 'Commodity', caption 'Description of the goods'). CONFIRMED from the trace: see navigation in openQuestions. This page ADVANCES; it does not return to the species-picker.
secondaryActions: Remove (inferred — per saved row; never rendered in any trace)

structure.text: NOT observed — no trace on this page rendered an error state. The template includes {{> common/validationSummary}} as the first child of the grid column, i.e. the error summary renders ABOVE the caption/h1.

govukComponents.usage: One table per genus/species (keyed by EPPO code) listing that species' variety/class pairs. The table <caption> holds an h2 with '<EPPO code> - <complement name>' plus the hidden 'Other' warning. Repeats — a notification with N species renders N tables. CORROBORATED (inferred) that N>1 is real: the bulk-upload fixture resources/file-upload/2-Commodity.csv:2-5 puts four species (MABAN, MABBA, MABBR, MABBV) under one commodity code 0808108090, which would render four tables on this page. No trace exercised the multi-table state.

Free-text 'Other' variety name, revealed by client JS only when the variety select = 'Other'. NOTE (newly confirmed): js-hidden is CSS-only — the field is still submitted with the form even while hidden, as an empty string. See the confirmed POST payload in fields > add-varietyother.

Section caption 'Description of the goods' above the H1 (id=page-secondary-title). Names the journey section this page belongs to. NOTE the destination page (/commodity/summary) carries the SAME caption — confirming both pages sit in the 'Description of the goods' section.

govukComponents.usage: Wraps each of the three controls in the entry row (variety select, Other input, class select). NOTE the non-standard part: each form group contains a control but NO govuk-label — the standard composition is form-group > label + control. See nonStandardPatterns.
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — independently re-verified: document.querySelectorAll('main .govuk-form-group').length === 3

govukComponents.usage: The third column header's 'Remove button' text (the column is visually blank), and the 'Warning' prefix inside the Other warning. The 'Remove button' header is the only accessible naming the remove column has.
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — re-verified: the three textContents are exactly 'Variety', 'Class', 'Remove button'; Warning in the warning text

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A <button type=submit> styled by bespoke IPAFFS CSS to look like a text link. Used for 'Add another variety', 'Add another Genus (and species)' and per-row 'Remove'. These are real form submits — each round-trips to the server to add/remove a row.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System component. The visual affordance (link) contradicts the semantics and behaviour (form submit that mutates the draft). Three separate submit buttons plus the primary means the handler must disambiguate intent by inspecting which button name was posted — now CONFIRMED as the mechanism: the observed POST payload carries 'button-save-and-continue=Save and continue' as an ordinary form field, so the server genuinely name-sniffs the payload to learn intent. NOTE: no page object models any of these three buttons and no test clicks them — the whole multi-row surface is unautomated.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button with govuk-button--secondary for add/remove actions, and the standard 'Add another' pattern from the GOV.UK 'Add another thing' pattern. The new app should model this as an explicit collection add/remove route rather than name-sniffing the payload.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 (id=add-CIDAC name=add-button-CIDAC class='link-button govuk-!-margin-top-2', textContent exactly 'Add another variety'; id=add-genus class='link-button'); POST payload request 905 (button-save-and-continue=Save+and+continue); varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:70,128,138; absence corroborated ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/CommodityClassPage.ts:1-13 (only 2 locators)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Back link with href='#' rendered alongside Breadcrumbs
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The page chrome puts a govuk-back-link ('Back', href='#') and a one-item govuk-breadcrumbs ('Dashboard') side by side inside a bespoke .navigation-links__left-block, separated by a bespoke .navigation-links__divider containing a literal '|' character. Both components carry bespoke coupling classes (back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs, breadcrumbs--next-to-back).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Three separate departures. (1) href='#' means the Back link does nothing without JavaScript — the Design System's back link should be a real link to the previous page so it works unenhanced; here it must be JS-driven (history.back()). (2) The Design System says to use EITHER a back link OR breadcrumbs, not both — they are competing navigation models, and the service has resolved the conflict with custom CSS rather than by choosing. (3) The '|' divider is a presentational character in the DOM, which screen readers may announce. NOTE: this is shared chrome, not unique to this page — but it is in scope because the page spec records a backLink.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Pick one: a govuk-back-link with a real href to the previous page (works with no JS), OR breadcrumbs. Delete the divider and the two coupling classes. A linear task journey like this wants the back link, not breadcrumbs.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence:
trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — .navigation-links__left-block outerHTML: Back
|
…Dashboard…

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: aria-label on #add-genus does not match its visible text
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The 'Add another Genus (and species)' button carries aria-label='Add Another Genus (and species)' — capital 'Another' — while its visible text content is 'Add another Genus (and species)' with a lowercase 'another'. The accessible name therefore differs from the visible label, and the aria-label is redundant in the first place (the button already has a text node).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A redundant aria-label overriding identical visible text is a code smell; the case divergence is evidence it has drifted. It scrapes past WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name only because that check is case-insensitive. It also silently changes what voice-control users must say.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Delete the aria-label entirely and let the visible text be the accessible name. Never add aria-label to a control that already has a visible text label.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — independently re-verified from the DOM: #add-genus getAttribute('aria-label') === 'Add Another Genus (and species)' while textContent.trim() === 'Add another Genus (and species)'; varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:138 — re-verified verbatim: <button class="link-button" id="add-genus" name="add-genus" value="Add another Genus (and species)" type="submit" aria-label="Add Another Genus (and species)">Add another Genus (and species)</button>

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: jQuery $(document).ready binds a change listener to every select[id^=add-variety] and toggles the js-hidden class on the matching 'Other' text input and warning, showing them only when the value is exactly 'Other'. It also calls showHideOther once on load to sync the initial state.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A hand-rolled conditional reveal on a <select>, driven by jQuery. It is NOT progressive enhancement — with JS off the 'Other' input stays hidden yet is still validated server-side, so a no-JS user selecting 'Other' cannot complete the page. (js-hidden is CSS-only: the field is still POSTed while hidden — confirmed in the observed payload as an empty add-varietyother-CIDAC — so a no-JS user would submit an empty value into a required field with no way to fill it.) The Design System deliberately provides conditional reveal for radios/checkboxes only, precisely because select-driven reveals are an accessibility problem. Unautomated: no test selects 'Other', so the reveal has never been exercised by QA either.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-radios with conditional reveal (if the variety list is short), or split 'Other' onto its own page. Either removes the jQuery dependency entirely. Do not port this widget.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 (js-hidden re-verified in the class attributes of #add-varietyother-CIDAC and #other-warning-CIDAC); POST payload request 905 (add-varietyother-CIDAC= submitted empty while hidden); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/assets/javascripts/variety_and_class.js:1-23

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Form controls with aria-label and no visible <label>
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Both selects and the 'Other' input carry only aria-label ('Select variety', 'Select class', 'Enter Other Variety'). The visible naming comes from the table column headers (Variety / Class), not from a label element.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A non-standard use of the govuk Select component — the Design System requires a visible <label> bound to the control. Screen-reader users get the aria-label; sighted users infer meaning from the column header. It also means the error summary has no visible label text to link back to. AGGRAVATING FINDING: this page is absent from tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts, which walks the CHED-PP journey page by page and calls checkPageAccessibility on each — so the one page in the journey with aria-label-only controls is the one page never axe-scanned.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-label per control (visually hidden if the column header carries the visible naming), or drop the table layout and use one labelled form group per variety/class pair.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — independently re-verified: document.querySelectorAll('main label').length === 0, and all three controls carry aria-label (add-variety-CIDAC='Select variety', add-varietyother-CIDAC='Enter Other Variety', add-class-CIDAC='Select class'); a11y gap corroborated ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:23-126 (no 'Variety'/'Class' page in the walk)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke wrapper div around the table, presumably giving horizontal overflow scrolling on narrow viewports.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom CSS outside the toolbox. govuk-frontend has no responsive-table wrapper because the Design System's guidance is to avoid tables that need one.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Restructure so the table fits, or use a Summary list per species. A 3-column table (Variety / Class / Remove) should not need overflow scrolling.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26; varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:40 — re-verified verbatim: <div class="table-responsive">

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Bespoke hook classes: species-variety, species-class, species-remove, species-variety-and-class--remove, text-align-right, no-wrap, commodity-details-lines-remaining, variety-of-genus-and-species-table, variety-of-genus-and-species-page, navigation-links__left-block, navigation-links__divider
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Page- and column-scoped hook classes, styled by service/src/assets/sass/pages/_variety_of_genus_and_species.scss — column widths, text alignment, page-specific tweaks. Plus the shared navigation-links__* chrome classes.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Per-page bespoke SCSS. Every one of these is a place the legacy service stepped outside govuk-frontend for layout it could have got from utility classes — note 'text-align-right' and 'no-wrap' in particular, which duplicate govuk-!- utilities outright.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-!-width-* / govuk-!-text-align-* utility classes on the table cells, or a Summary list which handles the label/value/action layout natively.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 (class inventory: variety-of-genus-and-species-page, variety-of-genus-and-species-table, species-variety, species-class, species-remove observed; navigation-links__left-block and navigation-links__divider re-verified in the chrome); varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:24,62,68-70; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/assets/sass/pages/_variety_of_genus_and_species.scss

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The 'Other' warning nests a Warning text component inside an Inset text component, and puts the whole thing inside the table's <caption> element alongside an h2.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Two attention components nested is not a documented Design System composition — it double-emphasises. Putting interactive/conditional content inside a table <caption> is also unusual and hurts the table's accessible name (the caption is announced as the table's name, so the warning text becomes part of it when revealed). Observed accessible name of the table while hidden: 'CIDAC - Citrus australasica'.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single govuk-warning-text (or better, a govuk-hint) placed in the form group next to the 'Other' input it relates to — not in the table caption.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 (main.html: <caption class=govuk-table__caption> contains h2 + div#other-warning-CIDAC.govuk-inset-text > .govuk-warning-text)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Dynamic field names keyed by EPPO code (add-variety-CIDAC, add-class-CIDAC, add-varietyother-CIDAC, add-button-CIDAC, remove-button-{uniqueComplementID})
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Field names are built by string concatenation with the EPPO code. The server re-derives the species list by scanning payload keys for the 'add-variety-' prefix and splitting on '-'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The form payload IS the data model. Validation parses field names to discover which species were on the page (validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:44 does key.split('-')[2]), which breaks for any EPPO code containing a hyphen. WORSE, AND INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED: the client and the server use two DIFFERENT string-surgery rules to recover the same EPPO code — the client does this.id.substring(this.id.lastIndexOf('-') + 1) (variety_and_class.js:3) while the server does key.split('-')[2] (variety_of_genus_and_species.js:44). For a hyphenated code these disagree (client takes the last segment, server takes the third), so the reveal would bind to one species while validation attributed the value to another. This is exactly the architecture the rebuild must not copy. NOTE the QA side is blind to this: because CommodityClassPage.ts locates the class select by its static aria-label ('Select class') rather than its dynamic id, the tests would not notice an EPPO-code-keyed naming regression at all.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No component fix — an architecture point. Model the collection server-side (species list held in the draft, addressed by index or id) and use stable, structured field names.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 (control inventory re-verified: names are exactly add-variety-CIDAC, add-varietyother-CIDAC, add-class-CIDAC, add-button-CIDAC); server derivation validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:44 — re-verified verbatim: '.map(key => key.split('-')[2])'; client derivation assets/javascripts/variety_and_class.js:3 — re-verified verbatim: 'let eppoCode = this.id.substring(this.id.lastIndexOf('-') + 1);'; locator strategy ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/CommodityClassPage.ts:6-8

fields.name: add-variety-{eppoCode} (observed: add-variety-CIDAC)
fields.label: Variety
fields.labelNote: There is NO <label> element on this page (independently verified: 0 labels in main). 'Variety' is the visible <th> column header; 'Select variety' is the control's aria-label and therefore its accessible name. The rebuild should give this control a real label.
observedValues: None (posted, not selected by any test — it was the pre-selected sole option)
fields.conditionalOn: One per genus/species on the commodity. The whole page is only reachable for commodities whose species carry variety/class detail. The table for a species is rendered ONLY if it has >0 variety options AND >0 class options (varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:38). CORROBORATED (inferred): tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:50-57 adds a species to commodity 06011010 (hyacinths) via btnAddSpecies, saves, and lands directly on About The Consignment — no variety/class page interposed. So the page's appearance is genuinely commodity/species-dependent and not merely gated by the test harness.
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — independently re-verified: <select id=add-variety-CIDAC name=add-variety-CIDAC class='govuk-select' aria-label='Select variety'> with exactly one option, value 'None' and text 'None'. POST payload request 905 confirms it posts 'add-variety-CIDAC=None'.
fields.requiredEvidence: MANDATORINESS IS TWO-LEVEL AND THEY DISAGREE. (1) FRONTEND (legacy): ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:49-54,58-69 makes variety CONDITIONALLY required — required when class was chosen for the row (SPECIES_VARIETY, :49-54) or when the species has zero saved entries (SPECIES_VARIETY_MANDATORY, :58-69); if entries already exist the empty row is Joi.any().optional() (:66-68). So 'required: true' holds only for the first row of a species; it is a UI-level Joi policy. (2) SUBMISSION MODEL (legacy): the backend schema stores variety as a keyDataPair entry (key 'variety', ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/.../representation/ComplementParameterSet.java:47) and applies NO @NotNull/@ChedppNotNull group to it — the only CHED-PP keyDataPair @NotNull/@MinValue validations are net_weight/number_package/type_package/type_quantity/quantity (ComplementParameterSet.java:63-133; ValidationMessages.properties:130-134). So a CHED-PP notification with NO variety passes submission validation. This is a policy the rebuild may deliberately revisit: variety is FE-mandatory-when-offered but model-optional, which is why the bulk-CSV route (blank variety) can reach 'Valid' (see openQuestions). Tag: requiredness legacy; the divergence is a finding, not confirmed rendered behaviour.
fields.notes: 'required: true' reflects the FRONTEND Joi policy (legacy, conditional — see requiredEvidence), NOT the submission model, which treats variety as an optional keyDataPair. The field's existence and options are confirmed; its mandatoriness is legacy/conditional. QA CORROBORATION FAILED — DELIBERATELY RECORDED AS A GAP. There is NO page object locator for the variety select anywhere in ipaffs-qa-automation (CommodityClassPage.ts:1-13 exposes only the class select and Save and continue), and no test in the suite ever selects a variety. The ChedPPNotificationConfig interface has no variety field at all (ched-pp-workflows.ts:55-75 lists firstEppoCode:61 and commodityClass:63, no variety). This field is driven by exactly zero automated tests. The only reason the observed journey passed without touching it is that CIDAC's list had a single option ('None') already selected. CORROBORATION FROM THE RISK ENGINE — variety is an IDENTIFIED reference-data entity, not a free string: utils/risk-engine.ts declares varietyId: string on all three rule payloads (SetCommodityImportRulePayload:22, SetCommodityHmiPayload:54, SetCommodityPhsiPayload:64), i.e. HMI, PHSI and import risk rules are targeted per-variety alongside commodityId and eppo. varietyId is '' in EXAMPLE_PHSI_COMMODITY_RULE (:100) meaning a rule can be variety-agnostic. ARCHITECTURE CONSEQUENCE for the rebuild: this page posts the variety NAME (the observed payload literally carries add-variety-CIDAC=None; the Other branch posts free text), yet the risk engine keys off a varietyId. The new app should capture and persist a variety identifier, not a display string. See openQuestions.

fields.name: add-varietyother-{eppoCode} (observed: add-varietyother-CIDAC)
fields.label: Enter Other Variety
fields.labelNote: aria-label only — no visible label and no column header of its own; it sits in the Variety cell beneath the variety select.
fields.conditionalOn: Revealed by client JS only when add-variety-{eppoCode} = 'Other'. Server-side, Joi.when(...is: 'Other') makes it required and pattern-checked; otherwise Joi.any().optional(). Present but js-hidden in the observed snapshot — and NEWLY CONFIRMED: still submitted (as an empty string) even while hidden, because js-hidden is CSS-only rather than a disabled/removed control.
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — independently re-verified: <input type=text id=add-varietyother-CIDAC name=add-varietyother-CIDAC class='govuk-input govuk-!-margin-top-4 js-hidden' aria-label='Enter Other Variety'>; POST payload request 905 carries 'add-varietyother-CIDAC=' (empty) despite the field being hidden; reveal logic assets/javascripts/variety_and_class.js:11-21; validation validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:71-86
fields.requiredEvidence: MANDATORINESS (legacy, conditional). ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:71-86 — required ONLY when the variety select = 'Other': Joi.when(variety, { is: OTHER, then: Joi.string().trim().empty('').regex(/^[a-z ]{1,32}$/).required(), otherwise: Joi.any().optional() }). At the submission-model level there is no separate 'other variety' field at all — the resolved free text is persisted as the keyDataPair 'variety' value (ComplementParameterSet.java:47), which carries no @NotNull group. So this is a FE-only conditional rule the rebuild may revisit.
fields.notes: The element and its empty submission are confirmed; 'required: true' is LEGACY and conditional (only when variety = 'Other' — see requiredEvidence). QA CORROBORATION FAILED. No page object locator, no test, no config field. The 'Other' branch is unautomated end-to-end — the reveal, the required rule and the lowercase-32-char pattern have never been exercised by a test OR rendered visible in a trace.

fields.name: add-class-{eppoCode} (observed: add-class-CIDAC)
fields.label: Class
fields.labelNote: 'Class' is the visible <th> column header; 'Select class' is the aria-label and the accessible name the test locates by (getByLabel('Select class')).
fields.conditionalOn: One per genus/species, in the same table row as its variety select. THIS IS THE 'Select class' CONTROL that CommodityClassPage.ts models — it lives on this page, not on a separate commodity-class page. Confirmed: the URL at the moment of selection is /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011/commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000. In journey terms the page is variant-only: ched-pp-workflows.ts:313 gates the whole page behind `if (commodityClass)`, and commodityClass is optional with DEFAULT_CONFIG value '' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:62-63, :147) — so the default CHED-PP journey never visits this page.
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — Select option, selector internal:label="Select class"i, log 'locator resolved to <select id="add-class-CIDAC" class="govuk-select " name="add-class-CIDAC" aria-label="Select class">', return values ['Extra Class']; option list independently re-verified from the DOM: ''=>'Select class', 'Class I'=>'Class I', 'Class II'=>'Class II', 'Extra Class'=>'Extra Class'; POST payload request 905 carries 'add-class-CIDAC=Extra+Class'; snapshot Page Title 'Variety of Genus (and Species) - Import and export applications - GOV.UK'; source workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:314; page object CommodityClassPage.ts:6-8; option-value corroboration types/commodity-class.ts:1-5
fields.requiredEvidence: MANDATORINESS IS TWO-LEVEL. (1) FRONTEND (legacy): ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:55-57 makes class Joi.string().empty('').required() -> SPECIES_CLASS ONLY when the row is being filled (variety OR class non-empty and action != REMOVE); otherwise class is Joi.any().optional() (:69). So class is required-when-the-row-is-active, not unconditionally. (2) SUBMISSION MODEL (legacy): class is a keyDataPair entry (key 'class', ComplementParameterSet.java:48) with NO @NotNull/@ChedppNotNull group — the model does not require it. Requiredness tag: legacy/conditional; a policy the rebuild may revisit.
fields.notes: 'required: true' is the FRONTEND Joi policy (legacy, conditional — see requiredEvidence); the submission model treats class as an optional keyDataPair. CORROBORATED. The placeholder option 'Select class' has value='' (empty) — re-verified — i.e. it is a non-answer; the three real options carry their label as their value. types/commodity-class.ts:1-5 declares the domain vocabulary as exactly { classI: 'Class I', classII: 'Class II', extraClass: 'Extra Class' } — matching the three real options in the trace. The tests treat class as a closed 3-value enum. This raises confidence that the class list is a fixed enum rather than per-species reference data, though the template still drives it from varietyAndClass.classOptions per species (see openQuestions). Only 'Extra Class' has ever been selected by a test (ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts) — 'Class I' and 'Class II' are declared but never used anywhere in the suite. LOCATOR STRATEGY NOTE: ched-pp-workflows.ts:314 selects by `selectOption({ label: commodityClass })` — matching the option's LABEL, not its value. Because the three real options happen to carry their label as their value, the tests cannot distinguish label from value and would not catch a divergence between them. FINDING — CLASS IS ABSENT FROM THE RISK ENGINE: a case-insensitive grep for 'class' across utils/risk-engine.ts returns nothing, while varietyId appears on all three rule payloads. So variety drives HMI/PHSI/import risk targeting and class demonstrably does not. That asymmetry is evidence class is captured for the certificate/marketing-standard record rather than for risk, and the rebuild should not assume the two fields have the same downstream consumers.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (hidden) crumb
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — <input name="crumb" type="hidden"> on the POST form (re-verified in the control inventory as the first field in main); template varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:19. Also present in the POST payload request 905. CSRF token. Value [REDACTED].
fields.notes: Infrastructure, not a user-facing requirement. Listed for parity with sibling page specs and because the rebuild needs an equivalent CSRF defence. Note the crumb is ALSO sent as a cookie (hapi crumb double-submit pattern) — value [REDACTED].

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (hidden) etag
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — <input name="etag" type="hidden" value=""0000000000BD2B51"">; POST payload request 905 carries 'etag=%220000000000BD2B51%22' (i.e. the quoted ETag "0000000000BD2B51", URL-encoded); template varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:20; schema spread ...ETAG_SCHEMA at validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:26
fields.notes: Optimistic-concurrency token round-tripped through the form so the server can reject a stale write. Note the value retains its HTTP ETag double quotes through the round trip. ARCHITECTURE POINT for the rebuild: the legacy page carries draft version state in the HTML form. The new app needs a deliberate answer on concurrent-edit conflict, not an accidental one.

fields.name: commodityDetailsPage
fields.label: (hidden) commodityDetailsPage
fields.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 26 — <input name="commodityDetailsPage" type="hidden" value=""> (empty in the observed state); POST payload request 905 carries 'commodityDetailsPage=' (empty); template varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:21; validation Joi.any().optional() at variety_of_genus_and_species.js:28
fields.notes: GAP — purpose unverified. Empty in the only observed render AND empty in the only observed submit, and validated as Joi.any().optional(), so nothing in the evidence says what it carries or who sets it. Suspected return-destination routing state. NOTE: it was empty on the submit that redirected to /commodity/summary, so /commodity/summary is the DEFAULT destination when this field is blank — what a non-empty value would do is still unknown. Needs a human answer before the rebuild copies or drops it.

postPayload.note: The complete, verbatim body of the observed 'Save and continue' POST — the whole data contract of this page in one line. Credentials redacted.
postPayload.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 request 905 — POST .../commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000, Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Content-Length 217, status 302
observations: The submit button is itself a payload field (button-save-and-continue=Save and continue) — the server learns intent by name-sniffing. Confirms the link-button concern in nonStandardPatterns.
The js-hidden 'Other' input IS submitted (empty). Hiding is CSS-only.
The variety posts a display NAME ('None'), not an id — directly relevant to the varietyId risk-engine mismatch.
Six of the seven fields are EPPO-keyed or infrastructure; only two carry user intent.

validationMessages.trigger: Variety left blank when a class HAS been chosen for that species (i.e. the user part-filled the row), and the action is not a Remove.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:46-54 (ANY_REQUIRED -> errorMessages.SPECIES_VARIETY, re-verified verbatim: variety Joi.string().empty('').required() when variety OR class non-empty and action != REMOVE); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:144 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_VARIETY: 'Select the variety',". Copy tagged legacy (read from the authoritative source string table); the trigger condition is read from the same handler.
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string. QA corroboration attempted and found nothing — grep for 'variety' across ipaffs-qa-automation returns only utils/risk-engine.ts (a varietyId API field) and resources/file-upload/2-Commodity.csv (a bulk-upload column). Remains source-only.

validationMessages.trigger: Class left blank when a variety HAS been chosen for that species.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:55-57 (class Joi.string().empty('').required(), ANY_REQUIRED -> errorMessages.SPECIES_CLASS — re-verified verbatim); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:143 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_CLASS: 'Select the class',"
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string. Copy is legacy; the class-required rule is FE-Joi-only (see the class field's mandatoriness note — the submission model does not require class).

validationMessages.message: At least one species variety must be added
validationMessages.trigger: Save and continue with the whole row empty AND no variety/class already saved for that species. If entries already exist, the empty row is allowed through (Joi.any().optional()).
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:58-69 — re-verified verbatim: when the row is empty (variety AND class blank) AND getVarietyAndClassDataFromNotification(...) returns 0 existing entries, variety Joi.string().empty('').required() -> errorMessages.SPECIES_VARIETY_MANDATORY; when existing entries > 0 the empty row is Joi.any().optional() and class is Joi.any().optional(); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:145 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_VARIETY_MANDATORY: 'At least one species variety must be added',"
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string. Note the else-branch is also entered when action === REMOVE_ACTION, so removing the last saved row appears to trip this rule too — unverified edge case.

validationMessages.message: Variety and class has already been entered for species
validationMessages.trigger: The submitted variety+class pair exactly matches one already saved for that species. For variety='Other', the comparison uses the 'Other' free-text value, not the literal 'Other'.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:88-105 (duplicatesValidationFunction — for variety='Other' the compared value is the ADD_VARIETY_OTHER_KEY free text, not the literal 'Other'; raises 'duplicate.variety_class'); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:146 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_VARIETY_DUPLICATE: 'Variety and class has already been entered for species',"
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string. Note the copy is ungrammatical ('has already been entered') and trails off without naming the species — the rebuild should not port it verbatim.

validationMessages.message: Enter the name of other variety
validationMessages.trigger: Variety = 'Other' but the free-text box is empty (after trim).
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:71-86 — re-verified verbatim: ADD_VARIETY_OTHER_KEY is Joi.when(variety, { is: OTHER, then: otherVarietyJoi(), otherwise: Joi.any().optional() }); otherVarietyJoi() = Joi.string().trim().empty('').regex(/^[a-z ]{1,32}$/).required() with ANY_REQUIRED -> SPECIES_VARIETY_OTHER_REQUIRED; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:147 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_VARIETY_OTHER_REQUIRED: 'Enter the name of other variety',"

validationMessages.message: Other variety must only contain lowercase characters a-z up to length 32
validationMessages.trigger: Variety = 'Other' and the free-text fails /^[a-z ]{1,32}$/ — i.e. anything with an uppercase letter, digit, hyphen or punctuation, or longer than 32 chars. Note the regex DOES permit spaces despite the message not saying so.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:82 (regex /^[a-z ]{1,32}$/) + :84 (STRING_PATTERN -> SPECIES_VARIETY_OTHER_INVALID) — re-verified verbatim; ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:148 — re-verified verbatim: "SPECIES_VARIETY_OTHER_INVALID: 'Other variety must only contain lowercase characters a-z up to length 32',"
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string. The message is also arguably wrong copy — it does not mention that spaces are allowed, and a user typing a real cultivar name (which are conventionally capitalised) would be rejected with no explanation of why. Also note the CSV bulk-upload route (resources/file-upload/2-Commodity.csv:1) has a free-text 'Variety' column with no stated constraint — if that route reaches the same model, the lowercase-only rule may be a UI-only constraint. Question for a human.

validationMessages.message: Commodity lines must be fewer than {maxCommodityLines + 1}
validationMessages.trigger: Adding this variety/class would push the notification past the commodity-line cap. Max is config-driven (constants.js:12 -> config.maxCommodityLines), so the number in the rendered message is environment-dependent.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/variety_of_genus_and_species.js:29-31 (Joi.any().invalid(true), ANY_INVALID -> MAX_COMMODITY_LINES); ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:106 — re-verified verbatim as a template literal: "MAX_COMMODITY_LINES: `Commodity lines must be fewer than ${parseInt(MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_VALUE) + 1}`," with MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_VALUE imported from ../../utils/constants at en.js:1; utils/constants.js:12
validationMessages.notes: No test asserts this string and no test approaches the cap. The rendered string is never literal — it interpolates the configured cap. NOTE: en.js:107-109 declare three FURTHER commodity-line messages that this page's handler does not reference — MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_REACHED (a function: maxRemaining => `Select a maximum of ${maxRemaining} genus and species`), MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_SINGLE ('You have added the maximum amount of commodity lines') and MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_EXCEEDED (`You have exceeded the maximum of ${MAX_COMMODITY_LINES_VALUE} commodity lines per notification`). They belong to sibling pages; recorded here only so a rebuild does not mistake them for this page's copy.

validationMessages.message: You can only add one more variety and class before reaching the maximum number of commodity lines
validationMessages.trigger: Not a validation error — an in-page advisory paragraph (#last-commodity-line-with-some-selected-remaining) shown when isLastCommodityLineWithSomeSelected. First branch of a three-way if/else-if, so it is mutually exclusive with the other two.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:23-26 — string re-verified verbatim from the template (#last-commodity-line-with-some-selected-remaining, first branch of the {{#if}}/{{else if}}/{{else if}})

validationMessages.message: You have reached the maximum amount of commodity lines however you can add the following variety and class
validationMessages.trigger: Advisory paragraph (#max-commodity-lines-with-some-not-selected) shown when isMaxCommodityLinesWithSomeNotSelected. Second branch of the three-way if/else-if.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:27-30 — string re-verified verbatim (#max-commodity-lines-with-some-not-selected, second branch)

validationMessages.message: You have added the maximum amount of commodity lines. You cannot add another variety and class
validationMessages.trigger: Advisory paragraph (#max-commodity-lines) shown when isMaxCommodityLines. Third branch of the three-way if/else-if.
validationMessages.evidence: AUTHORISED LEGACY SOURCE. ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:31-35 — string re-verified verbatim (#max-commodity-lines, third branch)
validationMessages.notes: Button suppression is NOT exclusive to this state. Both 'Add another variety' (:127-131) and 'Add another Genus (and species)' (:137-139) are wrapped in {{#unless (or isLastCommodityLineWithSomeSelected isMaxCommodityLinesWithSomeNotSelected isMaxCommodityLines)}} — so ANY of the three advisory states hides BOTH add buttons. Separately, the empty input row is suppressed by a different condition again: {{#unless (and @root.isMaxCommodityLines (isVarietyAndClassSet varietyAndClass))}} (:76 — re-verified verbatim), i.e. only at the hard cap and only for species that already have a variety/class set.

openQuestions: THE EVIDENCE POINTER WAS CORRECT. Action 26 is on this page: URL .../commodity/variety-of-genus-and-species?commodity-selected-code=08059000, Page Title 'Variety of Genus (and Species) - Import and export applications - GOV.UK', H1 'Variety and class of commodity' — all three independently re-verified. CONFIRMED: 'Select class' is a control on THIS page, not a separate commodity-class page. CommodityClassPage.ts is a page object over a fragment of this page — the new app should not model commodity-class as its own page. QA CORROBORATION STRENGTHENS THIS: CommodityClassPage.ts:1-13 contains only two locators (the class select and Save and continue) and no URL/goTo method, which is exactly the shape of a fragment page object, not a page. The commodity code in the trace (08059000) matches types/commodity-code.ts:10 hmiOnlyCIDAC and the EPPO code matches types/eppo-code.ts:2 cidac — the trace is notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:15 '[IMTA-20591] CHED-PP HMI auto-completion › should auto-complete the HMI check outcome when risk decision is no inspection'.

RESOLVED — NAVIGATION. THIS SUPERSEDES AND REVERSES THE PRIOR 'GAP — NAVIGATION' ENTRY IN THIS SPEC, WHICH WAS WRONG. The prior spec inferred, from the QA workflow's double commodityBasicDescription save, that 'Save and continue here returns to Commodity Basic Description rather than advancing'. That inference is REFUTED by the trace, which captured the answer directly. CONFIRMED: the Save and continue POST returns 302 with Location: /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011/commodity/summary, and the follow-on GET 200 renders <title>Commodity - Import and export applications - GOV.UK</title> with id="page-primary-title">Commodity and id="page-secondary-title">Description of the goods. So this page ADVANCES to the CHED-PP Commodity summary page. It is demonstrably NOT the species-picker returned to: the species-picker rendered <title>Select species of commodity - Import and export applications - GOV.UK</title> (verified at action 25) — a different title, hence a different page. WHY THE PRIOR INFERENCE MISFIRED, and the lesson for anyone reading the QA repo as evidence: CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.btnSaveAndContinue is defined as a bare getByRole('button', { name: 'Save and continue' }) with no URL scoping (CommodityBasicDescriptionPage.ts:32-34), so it matches the Save and continue button on ANY page. The 'extra save' at ched-pp-workflows.ts:317 is therefore firing on the /commodity/summary page, not on Commodity Basic Description — the page-object NAME misled, not the flow. TAKEAWAY FOR THE REBUILD: the commodity leg is species-picker -> (variety/class, if the species has reference data) -> commodity summary. GENERAL TAKEAWAY: unscoped page-object locators make the QA repo an unreliable witness for navigation; prefer the 302 Location header.

COVERAGE IS THIN, AND THE QA REPO EXPLAINS EXACTLY WHY. Only ONE test in the entire suite sets commodityClass (ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts), and the workflow gates this whole page behind `if (commodityClass)` (ched-pp-workflows.ts:313 — re-verified) with DEFAULT_CONFIG commodityClass: '' (ched-pp-workflows.ts:147). One test that opts in => one trace. This page is not thinly covered by accident; it is opt-in-only in the test harness.

THE VARIETY SELECT IS COMPLETELY UNAUTOMATED. There is no page object locator for it anywhere in ipaffs-qa-automation, no ChedPPNotificationConfig field for it (ched-pp-workflows.ts:50-70 lists firstEppoCode and commodityClass but no variety), and no test selects a variety. Half of this page's namesake function has zero automated coverage. The only reason the observed journey works is that CIDAC's variety list had a single pre-selected option ('None'). A human should confirm whether variety is genuinely optional in practice, or whether this is an untested requirement.

THIS PAGE IS ABSENT FROM THE ACCESSIBILITY SUITE. tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:19-176 walks the CHED-PP journey calling checkPageAccessibility on ~25 pages (Notification Dashboard, Import Type, Country of Origin, Origin of Import, Description of Goods, Search Commodity, Commodity Basic Description, About The Consignment, Notification Hub, Commodity Extended Description, Transport Details, Goods Movement Services, Contact Details, Nominated Contacts, Accompanying Documents, Document Upload, Traders Addresses, Consignor Creation/Confirmation, Review Notification) and never reaches Variety/Class. The one page in the journey whose form controls have aria-label and no visible <label> (independently verified: 0 <label> elements in main) is the one page never axe-scanned. Strong candidate for a real, live a11y defect in IPAFFS today. The mechanism: the a11y spec does not call createChedPPNotification at all. It hand-walks the page objects, and at :44-52 it searches commodity 06011010 (hyacinths) and clicks btnAddSpecies.first() WITHOUT ever entering an EPPO code, then saves at :53 and lands on About The Consignment at :55-57. So the page is skipped because the a11y walk never picks a species with variety/class reference data — meaning simply setting commodityClass in the a11y test would not fix the coverage hole; the walk would also need an EPPO-code species like CIDAC.

FINDING — THE 'Add another Genus (and species)' BUTTON'S ACCESSIBLE NAME DOES NOT MATCH ITS VISIBLE TEXT. aria-label='Add Another Genus (and species)' vs visible text 'Add another Genus (and species)' — both re-verified directly from the DOM. Redundant aria-label on a control that already has visible text, and it has drifted in case. Delete the aria-label in the rebuild. See nonStandardPatterns.

FINDING — THE BACK LINK IS INERT WITHOUT JS AND SITS NEXT TO A BREADCRUMB. Back, followed by a bespoke '|' divider div and a one-item 'Dashboard' breadcrumb. The Design System says use a back link OR breadcrumbs, not both, and a back link should have a real href so it works unenhanced. This is shared page chrome rather than something unique to this page, so the rebuild should settle it once at layout level — but a page spec that records backLink: 'Back' should record that the link does nothing without JavaScript. See nonStandardPatterns.

FINDING — CLIENT AND SERVER DERIVE THE EPPO CODE FROM THE FIELD NAME BY DIFFERENT RULES. variety_and_class.js:3 uses this.id.substring(this.id.lastIndexOf('-') + 1); variety_of_genus_and_species.js:44 uses key.split('-')[2]. Both re-verified verbatim. For any EPPO code containing a hyphen these return different species. Neither is tested. This is the sharpest single piece of evidence that 'the form payload is the data model' is a real defect and not just an aesthetic complaint.

AN ALTERNATE INPUT PATH EXISTS FOR THIS DATA, AND A TEST ASSERTS IT SUCCEEDS WITH VARIETY AND CLASS BLANK. resources/file-upload/2-Commodity.csv:1 has 'Variety' and 'Class' columns in the CHED-PP bulk-upload fixture; both are EMPTY for all five rows (:2-6). This is now more than a fixture observation — the full round trip is test-asserted. ched-pp-workflows.ts:278-285 selects the 'Upload from CSV' radio on Description of Goods, uploads 2-Commodity.csv, waits for the upload-successful banner (:284) and confirms (:285) — then SKIPS the whole species/variety/class flow, going straight to About The Consignment (:286-288). tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-notification-csv.spec.ts:11-33 drives that path and asserts the notification is submitted, receives a CHED-PP reference, and is decided 'Valid' (`await expect(pages.decisionHub.locatorStatusLabel).toHaveText('Valid')`, :23), plus a Dynamics record exists (:26-32). So a CHED-PP notification for four Malus species with NO variety and NO class reaches Valid. That is direct tension with this page's server-side 'At least one species variety must be added' rule (inferred). Two readings, and a human must pick one: (a) variety/class are mandatory-only-when-offered, i.e. the Malus species carry no variety/class reference data so the rule never fires for them — which would make the CSV evidence consistent but says nothing about optionality; or (b) the mandatory rule is a UI-only constraint that the bulk route bypasses, i.e. a genuine two-doors-one-model inconsistency. Reading (a) is more likely given the a11y walk shows hyacinths also skip the page, and given the template gates the whole species table on varietyOptions.length > 0 AND classOptions.length > 0 (:38) — but nothing in the admissible evidence settles it. THE REBUILD NEEDS ONE ANSWER, NOT TWO — and if (b) is true, the legacy service can hold notifications that the manual journey would have refused to create.

FINDING — VARIETY IS A RISK-BEARING, IDENTIFIED ENTITY; CLASS IS NOT. utils/risk-engine.ts declares `varietyId: string` on all three rule payloads — SetCommodityImportRulePayload:22, SetCommodityHmiPayload:54, SetCommodityPhsiPayload:64 — so import, HMI and PHSI rules are all targeted per-variety, alongside commodityId and eppo. EXAMPLE_HMI_COMMODITY_RULE:91 and EXAMPLE_PHSI_COMMODITY_RULE:100 show varietyId can be a placeholder or '' (variety-agnostic rule). Meanwhile a case-insensitive grep for 'class' across the whole of risk-engine.ts returns NOTHING. Three consequences the rebuild must face: (1) variety is not decorative — it changes the inspection rate a consignment gets, which makes the fact that no test ever selects a variety a materially riskier coverage hole than it first appears; (2) variety has an ID in the risk domain but this page posts a NAME — now confirmed verbatim in the payload as add-variety-CIDAC=None, and the 'Other' branch posts unconstrained free text — so the new app should capture and persist a variety identifier from reference data, not a display string, or the risk join is done on a label; (3) variety and class are NOT symmetric fields despite sharing a row in this table — class appears to be captured for the certificate/marketing-standard record, variety for risk. The legacy UI's table presents them as a matched pair, which may be a UI fiction. Question for a human: confirm variety->varietyId provenance and what consumes class.

FINDING — THE SUITE'S ENTIRE EPPO VOCABULARY IS ONE CODE. types/eppo-code.ts:1-5 declares exactly `{ cidac: 'CIDAC' }`. Every EPPO-driven assertion in the CHED-PP suite runs through that single species. This makes two of this spec's sharpest risks STRUCTURALLY untestable as the harness stands: the hyphenated-EPPO-code defect (client and server derive the code from the field name by different rules) cannot be reproduced because no hyphenated code exists in the vocabulary, and the multi-table repeat state cannot be driven through the manual journey because the config exposes only firstEppoCode/secondEppoCode/thirdEppoCode (ched-pp-workflows.ts:61,64,66) with no way to attach two species to ONE commodity. The four-species CSV fixture is the only multi-species evidence in the repo and it bypasses this page entirely. If the rebuild wants regression cover here, the vocabulary needs widening first.

MULTI-SPECIES IS REAL EVEN THOUGH NO TRACE SHOWS IT. resources/file-upload/2-Commodity.csv:2-5 puts four species (MABAN, MABBA, MABBR, MABBV) under a single commodity code 0808108090. On this page that would render four tables, each with its own variety/class row and its own EPPO-keyed field names. The repeat-table structure is therefore a genuine requirement, not a theoretical one — but it has never been rendered in our corpus nor driven by a test. Independently re-verified that the observed render had exactly ONE table.

NEVER OBSERVED — the 'Other' variety flow. add-varietyother-{eppoCode} + the 'Please ensure this variety is not in the dropdown list' warning are in the DOM but js-hidden throughout. No trace selects variety='Other' AND no test does either. The reveal behaviour, the lowercase-only 32-char rule and both 'Other' error messages are unverified against rendered reality and unasserted by any human. Note the warning copy IS verbatim-verified as a string in the DOM — what is unverified is it ever being visible. Also note the hidden input is nonetheless submitted empty on every save (confirmed in the payload), so the server sees the field on every request regardless.

NEVER OBSERVED — the multi-row state. The observed table had zero saved rows. How a saved variety/class pair renders (read-only text cells + a 'Remove' link-button per row, per varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:65-75) is inferred from the template only. 'Add another variety' and 'Add another Genus (and species)' were never clicked in any trace and are modelled by no page object.

NEVER OBSERVED — every error state. No trace on this page rendered .govuk-error-summary (independently verified: document.querySelectorAll('.govuk-error-summary').length === 0 at action 26), and QA corroboration found NO test asserting ANY error message on this page. All 10 messages in validationMessages remain source-only, though every string has now been verified verbatim against en.js / the template at the exact cited line. What is unverified is the rendered form: how the error summary links to controls that have no visible <label>, and whether inline errors render inside table cells at all.

NEVER OBSERVED — the max-commodity-lines states. All three advisory paragraphs and the exceedsMaxCommodityLines error are conditional on a cap that is config-driven (config.maxCommodityLines), so even the rendered number is environment-dependent. No test approaches the cap. Product needs to confirm the intended cap for the new app.

VARIETY OPTIONS ARE REFERENCE DATA. add-variety-CIDAC had exactly ONE option ('None') — independently re-verified as value 'None', text 'None' — so this species has no real varieties, and the template's topOption='Select variety' placeholder was suppressed by the varietyOptions.length==1 branch (varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:79 — re-verified verbatim: {{#eq varietyAndClass.varietyOptions.length 1}}). The QA repo has no variety vocabulary type (types/ has commodity-class.ts, commodity-code.ts, eppo-code.ts, country.ts, package-type.ts etc., but nothing for variety), which corroborates that variety is dynamic reference data rather than a closed enum. We still have no evidence of what a populated variety list looks like, where 'Other' sits in it, or how big it gets. Note the length==1 suppression means a single-option list has NO empty placeholder — 'None' is pre-selected and posts the literal string 'None' (confirmed in the payload), which the rebuild must not mistake for 'no answer'. The new app should source variety options from a reference-data service.

CLASS OPTIONS — CORROBORATION IS NOW STRONG BUT NOT CONCLUSIVE. types/commodity-class.ts:1-5 declares exactly three values (Class I / Class II / Extra Class), the same three the trace rendered (re-verified), and the QA repo models class as a closed enum — unlike variety, which has no type at all. That asymmetry is itself evidence that the team treats class as fixed and variety as reference data. HOWEVER the template still drives class from varietyAndClass.classOptions per species, and only CIDAC was ever observed. Confirm with product whether class is a genuinely fixed 3-value list (EU marketing standards) or per-species. Note 'Class I' and 'Class II' are declared in the enum but selected by no test.

ARCHITECTURE FLAG for the rebuild: this page derives its own data model from the HTTP payload — validation scans payload keys for the 'add-variety-' prefix and does key.split('-')[2] to recover the EPPO code (validation/.../variety_of_genus_and_species.js:44). That breaks for any EPPO code containing a hyphen, and it means the form is the schema. The new app must hold the species collection server-side. Worth noting the QA suite could not catch a regression here: CommodityClassPage.ts:6-8 locates by the static aria-label 'Select class', never by the EPPO-keyed id.

GAP: no trace shows what happens after Save and continue from this page when a species still has an empty row but existing saved entries — the validator permits it (lines 65-68). Is a species allowed to end up with a partially-filled row silently discarded?

GAP — the hidden commodityDetailsPage field. Empty in the only observed render AND the only observed submit, and Joi.any().optional(). Because the destination on that submit was /commodity/summary, we know /commodity/summary is the default when the field is blank — but what a populated value does, and who populates it, is unverified. Suspected return-destination routing state (e.g. deep-linking back from a check-answers page). A human must say before the rebuild copies or drops it.

GAP — the hidden etag field means this page participates in an optimistic-concurrency scheme whose conflict behaviour is entirely unobserved. No trace shows a stale-etag rejection. The rebuild should decide deliberately how concurrent edits to one draft behave.

LEGACY MANDATORINESS RESOLVES THE CSV TENSION TOWARDS READING (a) — BUT LEAVES A REBUILD POLICY DECISION. New from the authoritative schema model: variety and class are stored as ComplementParameterSet keyDataPair entries (keys 'variety'/'class', ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/.../representation/ComplementParameterSet.java:47-48) and NEITHER carries a @NotNull/@ChedppNotNull group — the CHED-PP keyDataPair validations cover only net_weight/number_package/type_package/type_quantity/quantity (ComplementParameterSet.java:63-133; ipaffs-notification-microservice/.../ValidationMessages.properties:130-134). So the SUBMISSION MODEL does not require variety or class for CHED-PP, which is exactly why the bulk-CSV notification with blank Variety/Class columns reaches 'Valid'. The 'At least one species variety must be added' rule is therefore a FRONTEND-only (Joi) constraint on the manual journey, not a model invariant — the two-doors-one-model asymmetry in reading (b) is real at the code level, even if in practice the Malus species carry no variety/class reference data (reading (a)). REBUILD DECISION: variety/class mandatoriness is a policy the new service must set deliberately, at the model level, rather than inherit IPAFFS's split (FE-mandatory-when-offered, model-optional).

LEGACY — NO COPY DISCREPANCY. The template's page title text is set via {{> common/pageTitle primaryTitle="Variety and class of commodity" secondaryTitle="Description of the goods"}} (varietyOfGenusAndSpecies.html:19) — matching the confirmed rendered H1 'Variety and class of commodity' and caption 'Description of the goods' exactly. All 10 validation-message strings and both advisory-paragraph sets also match their authoritative source verbatim. No legacy-vs-rendered disagreement was found on this page.
